claim: Papers are becoming less important than implementations; applied researchers care about whether research is implementable, not just paper quality or novel ideas
actionable: When evaluating AI research, prioritize implementation difficulty and real-world usability over paper novelty; surface research that has working code and Docker containers available
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: Archive receives 30,000+ papers per month; only the top 0.1% (about 24 papers) are actually implementable or actionable for applied researchers
actionable: Use social signals and implementation metadata to rank papers rather than semantic search alone; focus tools on surfacing the tiny fraction of papers that practitioners will actually use
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: DeepSeek is the best bang-for-buck OCR solution when self-hosted on A100s with proper batching; Mistral APIs are more expensive but offer convenience
actionable: For bulk PDF-to-text parsing at scale, self-host DeepSeek OCR on your own infrastructure; use Mistral APIs only if you can't manage infrastructure
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: When ranking papers by implementation difficulty, you incentivize authors to provide working code and Docker containers; this creates a positive feedback loop for research quality
actionable: Publish paper implementation rankings; make 'implementation ease' visible to authors; watch as they naturally improve their code availability and reproducibility
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: Semantic search alone on 2.4M archive papers is too noisy; you must weight results by social signals (view count, comment engagement, Twitter mentions) to surface relevant work
actionable: When building a research search tool, combine semantic similarity with engagement metrics; don't rely on vector search alone
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: Gemini's 1M context window enables AI to answer detailed questions about entire papers by ingesting the full PDF and diagrams; Claude also supports diagram parsing
actionable: Use large-context models (Gemini, Claude) to directly ingest full paper PDFs with diagrams; enable AI to answer paper-specific questions without manual summarization
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: ICLR 2025 had 20% AI-generated reviews in peer review; exponential paper growth is collapsing conference quality and forcing an overhaul of academic review processes
actionable: Recognize that traditional peer review is becoming unreliable; invest in better signal mechanisms (implementation-based ranking, multi-reviewer consensus, AI-assisted review tools)
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: Author-written comments and early engagement on papers (from researchers like Laura from LLaMA) drive discovery better than algorithmic recommendation alone
actionable: Actively engage authors to write public comments on their own papers; surface papers with author participation prominently in feeds
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: Beating Hugging Face Papers required obsessing over UI/UX and directly embedding author participation; a better interface with author comments outperformed a larger platform's paper integration
actionable: When competing with established platforms, invest in interface polish and author engagement over raw feature count; small UX improvements can drive adoption better than breadth
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: ML research automation (Agent Laboratory) is easier to automate than other sciences; be skeptical of 'AI for science' claims unless they result in actual tool adoption by practitioners
actionable: When evaluating AI-for-science research papers, distinguish between marketing hype and actual tool usage; look for evidence of practitioners using the tool regularly
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv

claim: AI should assist research workflows (literature review, experiment design) rather than attempting to replace the entire scientific process; think 'virtual lab' not 'AI scientist'
actionable: When designing AI for science, focus on augmenting specific bottlenecks (literature review, experimental setup) rather than promising end-to-end automation
source_title: [State of AI Papers 2025] Fixing Research with Social Signals, OCR & Implementation — Team AlphaXiv
